differential_diagnoses: Nutritional optic neuropathy (B-vitamin deficiency, e.g., B1, B2, B3, B6, B12, or folate)
Demyelinating optic neuritis (MOGAD, MS, or NMOSD)
next_steps: Serum vitamin A level (retinol) and retinol-binding protein level
Comprehensive nutritional profile (Vitamin B1, B2, B6, B12, folate, copper, zinc, Vitamin D, Vitamin E)
Ophthalmology consultation for detailed dilated fundus examination and electroretinography (ERG)
Complete blood count with peripheral smear to check for anemia and hematologic manifestations of malnutrition
Dietary consultation and initiation of high-dose oral or intramuscular Vitamin A replacement
Referral to behavioral feeding therapy and low-vision rehabilitation services

differential_diagnoses: Vitamin A deficiency (Xerophthalmia and nutritional optic neuropathy)
Multiple nutritional deficiencies (B-vitamins, Copper, Vitamin C/Scurvy)
Demyelinating optic neuritis (MOGAD, MS, or NMOSD)
Refractive error due to severe corneal xerosis/keratomalacia
next_steps: Serum vitamin A (retinol), retinol-binding protein, and Vitamin C levels
Comprehensive trace element and vitamin panel (B1, B2, B6, B12, folate, copper, zinc, Vitamin D, Vitamin E)
Ophthalmology consultation for dilated fundus examination, retinal imaging, and electroretinography (ERG)
Complete blood count with peripheral smear and iron studies
Urgent nutritional supplementation (starting with Vitamin A, B-complex, and Vitamin C) under specialist guidance
Dietary evaluation by a registered dietitian and initiation of behavioral feeding therapy
Low-vision rehabilitation and Massachusetts Commission for the Blind registration
